body: Good evening, Deaf programmer from New Zealand here, checking out your library for first time. I'm impressed ... Just curious, why is there no ButtonGroup component when there is one for Checkboxes? Curious here ... PS: I wanted to post this under discussions, but couldn't find a button to start a discussion. Perhaps deactivated on your side?

comments.body: We have a [toggle group](https://base-ui.com/react/components/toggle-group), is that what you're looking for? @binarykitchen

Ah yes, that.

I come from a MUI world and there it's called ButtonGroup:
https://mui.com/material-ui/react-button-group/

But they also provide a ToogleGroup there. A bit confusing 😅

The reason is that "button group" (non-toggles) is just a `role="group"` around some normal buttons, so there isn't anything that a headless lib could add

ToggleGroup has toggle buttons, but also roving focus (keyboard nav). You could also make a "button group" with the [`Toolbar`](https://base-ui.com/react/components/toolbar) component, which supports regular buttons as well as toggle buttons.

Ah, interesting, we are getting closer. Yep, toolbar might be what I'm after. Although I'm a bit but unsure which Toolbar exports to use. `Toolbar.Root`, `Toolbar.Button` or `Button`? In other words, here's a real example: How would you implement those "Resume" and "Stop" button using Base-UI if I may ask?

Since it's only 2 buttons (2 tab stops) roving focus doesn't seem necessary, they could be `<Button>` components (possibly in a generic `div role="group"`)

If the buttons are disabled depending on the playback status (e.g. disable "Resume" when playing) the [focusableWhenDisabled](https://base-ui.com/react/components/button#Button-focusableWhenDisabled) prop can be used so maintain tab order and keep the button discoverable by SRs

Great input @mj12albert - I think I'll go for this.

Last question: it's a component library, unstyled, although I'd like to have some "natural" spacings between those two buttons without the need for styling and CSS (so that I can test it on Storybook).

Is there some component providing spaces between buttons or a native HTML tag which does this?

Thanks for your feedback.

> Is there some component providing spaces between buttons

You could probably make something like Material UI's [Stack component](https://mui.com/material-ui/react-stack/#direction) (or some libraries just call it `<Flex>`)

Thanks, but I'd rather not mix base-ui with Material UI.
This to keep it unstyled while there is still a gap between those two buttons.

In other words, is there some native HTML markup providing some gaps between HTML elements like buttons without CSS?

> In other words, is there some native HTML markup providing some gaps between HTML elements like buttons without CSS?

Is there some specific reason why you don't want to use CSS?
HTML is just functional, CSS is for looks.
Some elements might have styles you could be looking for, but are you sure about their stability? Because default styles for elements are browser-dependent.
That's why in the majority of cases, applications tend to apply a CSS Reset to start from a blank slate and aim to reach uniform styling regardless of the browser.

If possible, I suggest using the default tools of the platform. 👍

Good questions @LukasTy

https://github.com/binarykitchen/videomail-recorder becomes a theme-less React component library for reuse. This wit Base UI components without themes (themeless).

It uses [Storybooks](https://storybook.js.org/) for visual tests

I don't want to apply a CSS reset. Stick to default HTML and CSS for stories. That's why I'm asking for some native spacings between HTML buttons using browser defaults without applying for a theme.

Another idea: perhaps crazy, should I pick some random Base UI theme for stories only but not to export these in the component library?

@binarykitchen it seems like your aim is similar to what Base UI is.
Our components don't have any theme or styles, except on a few occasions where it is necessary to ensure functionality.

You can do the same, use whatever styles you want for documentation, but don't ship them with your components.
It depends on your expected users' usage. 😊

Great, thanks @LukasTy - that's the same what I think. Now that we're aligned ...

>  it seems like your aim is similar to what Base UI is.
Yup, I totally like your idea. Keep going 💪 

... may I ask if Base UI offers a simple HTML theme/style, installable via any package manager like `npm`?
Something like `npm install --save-dev @base-ui-components/storybook` which comes with some browser default spacings without being shipped to production.

> browser default spacings

I am surprised that there are no default spacings nor margins between buttons in Mozilla nor Google Chrome (without a CSS reset). Shouldn't there be one?

Google AI recommends

```html
<button>Button 1</button>
   
<button>Button 2</button>
```

Which is probably more accessible rather than a Base UI theme for storybook?
If so, then no such `@base-ui-components/storybook` would be necessary, hmmm ... has this ever been raised before?

Just raising. I think the accessible way with HTML spaces is better than a custom theme. What do you think? Has this been raised before?

@binarykitchen Base UI is a headless library, there are no styles or themes that we provide.
Having themes has been (https://github.com/mui/base-ui/issues/2292) raised a few times, but for now it is not on our agenda.
We feel like increasing the component portfolio would bring more value than delving into theming.

Your suggested approach with the non-breaking spaces seems like a nice and quick solution for a demo.
I have used it multiple times in various cases. 👍

> Your suggested approach with the non-breaking spaces seems like a nice and quick solution for a demo.
I have used it multiple times in various cases. 👍

Yep. It's actually funny to see how we forget the HTML basics like ` ` for basic examples like these without the need to add extra themes nor CSS 😅

Indeed. Usually, the simplest solution is the most brilliant one. 👌 

Related to this issue, is there anything else we should do?
Or can we close it?

Aye, let's close it for now. The simplest solutions are brilliant indeed ✌️ 

>  ... is there anything else we should do?

I just started to warm up with Base UI recently as a MUI old-timer 🫢

labels.name: support: question
labels.description: Community support but can be turned into an improvement.

title: Why is there no Button Group when you have a Checkbox Group?
